decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses rRNA markers to infer tick phylogeographical structure and re-evaluates an earlier inferred deep population structure.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicitly reports that McLain et al.'s earlier population structure was erroneous because fungal sequences contaminated the phylogenetic analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses complete PVY genomes and Bayesian analyses to evaluate geographic effects on viral population structure and dynamics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogeographic application to the worldwide diversification of potato virus Y.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Combines pathogen phylogenetics, haplotype networks, and sampling geography to infer geographic population differentiation and repeated host-range expansion.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to the geographic and host-associated structure of powdery mildew populations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes viral genetic variation and the association between phylogeny and geography among Iberian and global vvIBDV strains.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to the geographic persistence and population structure of infectious bursal disease virus.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The minireview summarizes VZV phylogenetics and phylogeography and advances an evolutionary hypothesis, but does not explicitly conduct, develop, diagnose, or implement the method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a broad evolutionary overview rather than a methodological lineage source or concrete analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies Bayesian analyses to worldwide PVY coat-protein sequences to assess geographic and host effects on population structure and dynamics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogeographic application to potato virus Y evolution.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses whole-genome sequencing, SNP genotyping, and phylogeographic analysis to infer the geographic origin of an anthrax outbreak strain.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic phylogeographic investigation of a human anthrax outbreak.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses HCV sequencing, phylogenetics, and coalescent analysis to reconstruct the age and historical spread of subtype 4e in Gabon.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete molecular phylogeographic application to hepatitis C dissemination.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares Rwandan HIV-1 sequences with geographically annotated neighboring-country sequences to assess regional phylogenetic connectivity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogeographic analysis of HIV-1 circulation in Kigali and surrounding countries.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Explicitly applies molecular phylogeographic analysis to test southern China's role in global influenza evolution.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to influenza introductions, persistence, and global spatial evolution.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Discusses the application of multiple statistical methods within a molecular-phylogeographic framework but does not explicitly introduce or conduct a specific analysis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological overview of Bayesian reconstruction, network parsimony, and regression for infectious-disease phylogeography.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs phylogeographic, phylodynamic, and spatiotemporal diffusion analyses of norovirus sequence data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to the global dissemination of an epidemic norovirus variant.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses an MLVA-based lineage classification and geographic stratification to construct a worldwide M. tuberculosis phylogeographic snapshot.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to global tuberculosis-complex diversity and human migration.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducts Bayesian phylogeographic analysis with a continuous-time Markov chain to model FMDV diffusion between Ecuadorian provinces.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete BEAST-based application to within-country viral spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies Bayesian phylogeographic analyses to envelope genes and full genomes to reconstruct DENV-4 introductions into Brazil.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete full-genome application to dengue-virus origins and multiple introductions.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Discusses the potential use of phylogeography in integrated zoonotic surveillance but does not report an actual analysis or new method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit guidance on adopting viral phylogeography in animal and public-health agencies.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian MCMC phylodynamic and phylogeographical analyses to reconstruct influenza introductions and geographic dispersion in Italy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to pandemic and post-pandemic A(H1N1)pdm09 spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes TBEV sequences from patients, rodents, and ticks to test correspondence between phylogenetic and geographic clustering.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete pathogen phylogeographic characterization across hosts and vectors in Slovenia.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Sequences Swedish F. tularensis isolates and relates genetic clades and temporal genotype variation to geographic locations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogeographic investigation of tularaemia source populations and historical spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses global MRSA genomic data to characterize geographic clustering and variation among phylogeographic subgroups.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete pathogen-genomic application across continental, national, and city scales.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses multilocus genotyping across Europe and Australia to assess whether C. jejuni population structure is associated with host or geography.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete analysis of phylogeographic population structure in a zoonotic pathogen.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs Bayesian phylogeographic analysis to identify epidemic links within and between countries for PCV2 recombinant clusters.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete viral phylogeographic application among Vietnamese pig farms and international sequences.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses partial genomes with Bayesian geographic analyses and discrete and continuous diffusion models to reconstruct African YFV dispersal.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application of Bayesian phylogeography and genomic diffusion models to yellow fever virus.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses microsatellite data from spatially and temporally sampled mosquito populations to assess geographic genetic structure and gene flow.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogeographic application to the vector of dengue in southeastern Florida.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reconstructs WNV-2 phylogeny on a spatiotemporal scale to estimate geographic origins, introductions, and dispersal.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to West Nile virus lineage movement from Africa into Europe.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses ribosomal and mitochondrial sequences from geographically sampled Ascaris populations to infer phylogenetic and phylogeographical relationships.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete parasite phylogeographic application addressing transmission between humans and pigs.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian sequence analysis to jointly estimate time-scaled phylogeny, demography, and ancestral spatial states of HCV subtypes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogeographic reconstruction of hepatitis C introductions and spread in Montenegro and the Balkans.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs phylogenetic and phylogeographic analyses to reconstruct introductions and differing spatial spread of two rabies-virus groups.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to the 2008–2011 fox-rabies epidemic in Italy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies phylogeographic analysis to metapneumovirus gene sequences to assess geographic restriction and global movement.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian MCMC analysis of rabies-virus sequences to reconstruct lineage locations, movements, dispersion, and diversification.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogeographic reconstruction of dog- and fox-associated rabies lineages in Brazil.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs Bayesian phylogenetic and phylogeographic analyses to reconstruct introductions and circulation of CTV lineages in Sicily.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Implements Bayesian phylogeography to estimate the spatial flow of TTSuV strains among Transylvanian counties.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes complete HBV genomes with a Bayesian coalescent and phylogeographic framework to infer introductions and directional spread.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete hepatitis B virus phylogeographic reconstruction in Brazil.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Bayesian phylogeography to infer ancestral areas and subsequent European spread of three novel porcine parvoviruses.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to emerging PPV2–4 species.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a Bayesian phylogeographic approach to distinguish in-situ rabies-virus evolution from repeated mainland introductions into Trinidad.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies phylogeographical techniques to determine the geographic source of a human rabies infection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies Bayesian phylogeographic and phylogeny-trait methods to 487 JEV sequences to test geographic and climatic structuring.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses MERS-CoV genomes and phylogeographic analyses to assess reservoir dispersion, introductions, and outbreak transmission.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete genomic phylogeographic investigation of MERS-CoV in Saudi Arabia.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies a newly developed MLVA typing system and Bayesian clustering to infer global and regional geographic population structure.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogeographic application to the invasive plant pathogen E. amylovora.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses whole H5N1 genomes to analyze demographic, adaptive, and spatial dynamics, including an explicit phylogeographic analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes 190 hantavirus sequences in BEAST to infer geographic origins and worldwide dissemination over time.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Combines Bayesian methods to reconstruct the origin, direction, velocity, and barriers of south-central skunk rabies spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a Bayesian discrete phylogeography model to infer H5N1 origins and dispersion routes among Egyptian governorates.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Examines geographically sampled hantavirus sequences and confirms geographic clustering of pathogenic human- and rodent-derived lineages.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses complete viral genomes, phylogenetics, and geographic sampling to characterize spatially differentiated Iranian CaMV populations.

decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete phylogeographic analysis of vesicular stomatitis outbreaks in Mexico and the United States.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses BEAST to estimate the emergence location, date, and spread across risk groups of HIV-1 CRF50_A1D.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes and implements a Bayesian epoch-model approach that relaxes time-homogeneity for continuous-time Markov-chain data, including phylogeographic traits.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses international RSV sequence comparisons to assess strain persistence, introductions, and geographic diversity in a Kenyan refugee camp.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses whole-genome typing and phylogenomics to place Haitian outbreak isolates within global geographic patterns and identify their source.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Explicitly proposes a novel methodology combining temporo-geospatial epidemiology with phylogeographic analysis to identify epidemic pathways.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a phylogeographic approach to estimate ancestral root locations for PPRV and its four lineages.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Quantifies inadequate geographic metadata and benchmarks four named-entity-recognition systems for recovering metadata needed by viral phylogeography.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses BEAST phylodynamic and phylogeographic analyses to reconstruct the origins and geographic spread of HIV subtypes in Uganda.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces the POTN heuristic algorithm for reconstructing partially observed transmission networks and compares it with BEAST and SeqTrack.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Establishes a novel short-p72 sequence analysis and epidemiologic-comparison protocol for genotyping and geographic source inference.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses 200 full EV-A71 genomes to describe Southeast Asian phylogeography and document viral gene-flow patterns.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The title names transmission-network extraction from phylogeographic data, but the empty abstract and title do not explicitly identify this record as a formal correction.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies phylogeographic analysis to RHDV genomes to identify Australian spatial structure and infer South Australia as a source population.

decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete hepatitis B virus phylogeographic application across Panamanian risk groups.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents a web-based workflow that links syndromic signals to sequence metadata and provides phylogeographic analysis and visualization functions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit software/workflow implementation supporting pathogen phylogeographic analysis and visualization.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces BASTA, a new BEAST2 structured-coalescent approximation for phylogeographic inference, and demonstrates it on Ebola data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies phylogenetic and Bayesian phylogeographic analyses to genomes and envelope sequences to reconstruct two DENV-2 introductions into Pakistan.
